topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink bled across the top of my exam paper, a stark, unforgiving slash across the 'B' I'd been anticipating. My stomach did a slow, unpleasant flip. I reread the feedback, the professor’s terse note about 'significant conceptual gaps,' and a hot flush crept up my neck, staining my cheeks a deep crimson. I quickly shoved the paper into my backpack, the sound of the zipper a loud, desperate rasp in the quiet library corner. Every glance from a passing classmate felt like a physical weight pressing down on my shoulders. I could feel my hands trembling slightly as I navigated the crowded hallway, desperately trying to become invisible, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole.

[story 2]

"A C-minus? Seriously?" I muttered under my breath, the sound barely audible over the low hum of the ventilation system. My jaw tightened, and I focused intensely on the scuffed toes of my sneakers, refusing to meet anyone’s gaze. The air in the lecture hall suddenly felt thick, suffocating. I could practically hear the silent judgment radiating from the rows ahead. When the professor announced the results, I felt a sudden, intense heat bloom across my face, and I instinctively hunched my shoulders inward, pulling my jacket tighter around myself as if trying to shrink into the fabric. I just wanted the ground to open up and let me disappear.

[story 3]

I stared at the printed grade, the number mocking me from the page. It wasn't just the score; it was the way everyone seemed to shift their posture, the subtle narrowing of eyes from the group huddled by the water fountain. A strange, hollow sensation settled in my chest, heavy and cold. I picked up my pen, not to correct anything, but just to grip it until my knuckles were white. "Well," I managed to say, my voice coming out thin and reedy, "that's... not what I was hoping for." The silence that followed was deafening, amplifying the frantic drumming of my pulse against my ribs. I wanted to melt into the linoleum floor, to somehow dissolve into the very structure of the classroom.
